parsed_review.summary: Read-only independent review of CP00-461 (RP15.P04.M05.S08-S17, risk_class A, 10 units, micro_title "Permission And Audit Binding", deliverable_distribution ui:3/security_audit:2/implementation:3/fixture:1/test:1). The pack is a descriptor-only layer: manifest reports production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false; production_ready_flag is the marker string "analytics_core_p04_permission_slice_descriptor_verified", not a boolean production/enterprise-trust claim, so the modest claim is honest and supported. Source inspection of packages/analytics/src/{registry,service,validators}.js confirms pure Object.freeze descriptor construction with no I/O, DB, network, permission/audit, UI, or API execution; all no_*_runtime and no-leak attestations (permission_decision_detail_excluded, audit_event_body_excluded, blocked_claim_detail_excluded, hermes_packet_body_excluded, fixture_payload_excluded, unauthorized_data_omitted, raw_payload_excluded) hold. command-evidence.json shows every gate at exit_code 0 (node --check, node --test, rp05/rp15 validates, closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/weighted/spec/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00 control-plane validates, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check) with Hermes gate H15 passed and no real data. validators.js builds the Hermes evidence packet (production_ready_candidate = coverage.valid && slice.valid, no_real_data:true) and Claude review packet (required_model claude-opus-4-8, read_only:true) deterministically for packet/receipt handling only — Hermes runtime is not embedded in product. Authority boundaries are enforced in code, validators, contract deepEqual, and tests: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, with human final approval still required and the enterprise trust claim closed outside this receipt. Review-evidence chain satisfies CP178+ hardening (readiness auth_preflight passed, 1449-byte valid-JSON stdout, no malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output, no missing required fields; prompt_bytes 2160 within limit; source_inspection_mode read_tools_used; summary_only_review correctly disallowed because security_audit deliverables are present). Changed-file scope matches the 7 active modified refs plus unchanged src/index.js; unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip json) are correctly excluded. One P3 documentation-level observation recorded; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout. This is a read-only reviewer recommendation only — it does not constitute final approval, which remains a human responsibility.

findings.title: security_audit rows are descriptor-only stubs with no permission/audit-specific fields
findings.details: ANALYTICS_CORE_CP461_ROW_EXTRAS spreads forward from the CP460..CP453 extras and contains no keys matching any CP461 row title, so the two security_audit rows for the 'Permission And Audit Binding' micro-title (Permission badge, Audit hint display) carry only the base descriptor fields (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false) with no permission- or audit-specific descriptor attributes. At the descriptor layer this is benign and consistent with prior packs CP453-CP460, and it does not affect coverage validation, the no-write/no-leak attestations, or the authority boundaries; recorded here only to document that mandatory source inspection of the security/audit deliverables occurred.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this descriptor-only pack. If a later pack promotes these permission/audit rows toward runtime, add permission/audit-specific descriptor fields and corresponding coverage assertions before any runtime or permission-decision binding is introduced.
